contentBlocks.original:
विश्वेश्वराय नरकार्णव तारणाय
कर्णामृताय शशिशेखर धारणाय ।
कर्पूरकान्ति धवलाय जटाधराय
दारिद्र्यदुःख दहनाय नमश्शिवाय ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśvēśvarāya narakārṇava tāraṇāya
karṇāmṛtāya śaśiśēkhara dhāraṇāya ।
karpūrakānti dhavaḻāya jaṭādharāya
dāridryaduḥkha dahanāya namaśśivāya ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Lord of the universe, the ship to cross the ocean of hell, nectar to the ears, wearing the crescent moon — of camphor-white radiance, bearing matted locks — salutations to Shiva who burns the sorrow of poverty.

contentBlocks.original:
गौरीप्रियाय रजनीश कलाधराय
कालान्तकाय भुजगाधिप कङ्कणाय ।
गङ्गाधराय गजराज विमर्धनाय
दारिद्र्यदुःख दहनाय नमश्शिवाय ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gaurīpriyāya rajanīśa kaḻādharāya
kālāntakāya bhujagādhipa kaṅkaṇāya ।
gaṅgādharāya gajarāja vimardhanāya
dāridryaduḥkha dahanāya namaśśivāya ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Beloved of Gauri, bearing the digit of the night-lord (moon), the ender of Kala, wearing a bracelet of the serpent king — bearer of the Ganga, slayer of the mighty elephant demon — salutations to Shiva who burns the sorrow of poverty.

contentBlocks.original:
भक्तप्रियाय भवरोग भयापहाय
उग्राय दुःख भवसागर तारणाय ।
ज्योतिर्मयाय गुणनाम सुनृत्यकाय
दारिद्र्यदुःख दहनाय नमश्शिवाय ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhaktapriyāya bhavarōga bhayāpahāya
ugrāya duḥkha bhavasāgara tāraṇāya ।
jyōtirmayāya guṇanāma sunṛtyakāya
dāridryaduḥkha dahanāya namaśśivāya ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Beloved of devotees, dispeller of the fear born of samsara-disease, the fierce one, ferrying souls across the dreadful ocean of worldly existence — of luminous form, of beautiful dancing forms and names — salutations to Shiva who burns the sorrow of poverty.

contentBlocks.original:
चर्माम्बराय शवभस्म विलेपनाय
फालेक्षणाय मणिकुण्डल मण्डिताय ।
मञ्जीरपादयुगलाय जटाधराय
दारिद्र्यदुःख दहनाय नमश्शिवाय ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
charmāmbarāya śavabhasma vilēpanāya
phālēkṣaṇāya maṇikuṇḍala maṇḍitāya ।
mañjīrapādayugaḻāya jaṭādharāya
dāridryaduḥkha dahanāya namaśśivāya ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Clothed in animal hide, body smeared with funeral ash, bearing a third eye on the forehead, adorned with gem-encrusted earrings — wearing anklets on both feet, bearing matted locks — salutations to Shiva who burns the sorrow of poverty.

contentBlocks.original:
पञ्चाननाय फणिराज विभूषणाय
हेमाङ्कुशाय भुवनत्रय मण्डिताय
आनन्द भूमि वरदाय तमोपयाय ।
दारिद्र्यदुःख दहनाय नमश्शिवाय ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pañchānanāya phaṇirāja vibhūṣaṇāya
hēmāṅkuśāya bhuvanatraya maṇḍitāya
ānanda bhūmi varadāya tamōpayāya ।
dāridryaduḥkha dahanāya namaśśivāya ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Five-faced, adorned with the ornament of the serpent king, bearing a golden goad, who glorifies all three worlds — bestower of the land of bliss, dispeller of darkness — salutations to Shiva who burns the sorrow of poverty.

contentBlocks.original:
भानुप्रियाय भवसागर तारणाय
कालान्तकाय कमलासन पूजिताय ।
नेत्रत्रयाय शुभलक्षण लक्षिताय
दारिद्र्यदुःख दहनाय नमश्शिवाय ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhānupriyāya bhavasāgara tāraṇāya
kālāntakāya kamalāsana pūjitāya ।
nētratrayāya śubhalakṣaṇa lakṣitāya
dāridryaduḥkha dahanāya namaśśivāya ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Beloved of the sun, the ship for crossing the ocean of samsara, the ender of Kala, worshipped by Brahma seated on the lotus — three-eyed, bearing auspicious marks — salutations to Shiva who burns the sorrow of poverty.

contentBlocks.original:
रामप्रियाय रघुनाथ वरप्रदाय
नागप्रियाय नरकार्णव तारणाय ।
पुण्याय पुण्यभरिताय सुरार्चिताय
दारिद्र्यदुःख दहनाय नमश्शिवाय ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāmapriyāya raghunātha varapradāya
nāgapriyāya narakārṇava tāraṇāya ।
puṇyāya puṇyabharitāya surārchitāya
dāridryaduḥkha dahanāya namaśśivāya ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Beloved of Rama (Vishnu), granter of boons to Raghunatha, beloved of serpents, the ship to cross the ocean of hell — the pure one, brimful of merit, worshipped by the gods — salutations to Shiva who burns the sorrow of poverty.

contentBlocks.original:
मुक्तेश्वराय फलदाय गणेश्वराय
गीताप्रियाय वृषभेश्वर वाहनाय ।
मातङ्गचर्म वसनाय महेश्वराय
दारिद्र्यदुःख दहनाय नमश्शिवाय ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
muktēśvarāya phaladāya gaṇēśvarāya
gītāpriyāya vṛṣabhēśvara vāhanāya ।
mātaṅgacharma vasanāya mahēśvarāya
dāridryaduḥkha dahanāya namaśśivāya ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Lord of liberation, granter of fruits, lord of Ganesha's retinue — beloved of sacred wisdom, whose vehicle is the bull, wearing an elephant hide — O Maheshvara, salutations to Shiva who burns the sorrow of poverty.

contentBlocks.original:
वसिष्ठेन कृतं स्तोत्रं सर्वरोग निवारणम् ।
सर्वसम्पत्करं शीघ्रं पुत्रपौत्रादि वर्धनम् ।

त्रिसन्ध्यं यः पठेन्नित्यं स हि स्वर्ग मवाप्नुयात् ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vasiṣṭhēna kṛtaṃ stōtraṃ sarvarōga nivāraṇam ।
sarvasampatkaraṃ śīghraṃ putrapautrādi vardhanam ।

trisandhyaṃ yaḥ paṭhēnnityaṃ sa hi svarga mavāpnuyāt ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: This stotra composed by Vasishtha destroys all diseases. It swiftly bestows all wealth and multiplies sons and grandchildren. One who recites it at the three sandhyas — morning, midday, and evening — will be freed from all poverty by the grace of Shiva. (Phalashruti)
